text.title: hybrid-search Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for hybrid-search as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using raw BM25 scores in linear combination without normalization.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: BM25 scores for a 10K-document corpus can range 0-30+; vector similarity scores range 0-1. Without min-max or z-score normalization, BM25 dominates entirely and linear combination degrades to pure BM25.

failure-mode.description: Two parallel network calls for manual fusion without timeout handling.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If vector DB is slow but BM25 is fast, waiting for both adds tail latency. Set a timeout on the slower call and fall back to the fast one's results alone.

failure-mode.description: Using Qdrant hybrid with a standard BM25 library instead of a sparse encoder (SPLADE).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Qdrant hybrid requires sparse vectors (e.g., SPLADE) as the sparse component — not the same as a Python BM25 library. Ensure the sparse encoder is deployed and version-pinned.

failure-mode.description: Fetching top-10 for fusion and then reranking the same 10.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The reranker can only reorder what it receives. Fetch top-100 for fusion, rerank the pool, return top-10. A relevant document at rank 15 is invisible to a reranker that receives only 10 candidates.

failure-mode.description: Score normalization for linear fusion is non-trivial: BM25 scores are unbounded; vector scores are 0-1; without proper min-max or z-score normalization, one signal dominates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Proper score normalization requires understanding the distribution of both signals across your corpus. It is dataset-specific and requires empirical tuning.

failure-mode.description: Alpha tuning is dataset-specific: the optimal alpha value for one corpus may perform poorly on another.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without empirical tuning on your specific query distribution and corpus, a fixed alpha may not be optimal. Consider implementing query-adaptive alpha selection.

failure-mode.description: RRF ignores actual score magnitudes: two documents with very different relevance scores but similar ranks get similar RRF scores.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This may not reflect true quality difference. If magnitude matters, switch to linear combination with proper normalization.

failure-mode.description: Reranking top-100 adds 50-100ms and 5x cost compared to retrieval alone.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Only enable reranking when precision matters more than speed.

failure-mode.description: BM25 tokenization matters: if BM25 uses a standard English tokenizer but the corpus contains code, product codes, or non-English text, keyword matching degrades significantly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always configure BM25's tokenizer to match your corpus characteristics.

failure-mode.description: Hybrid search from separate stores (manual RRF) has higher failure surface than native hybrid (Weaviate/Qdrant built-in).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two network calls = two failure modes. Native hybrid avoids this.
